title: AI uncertainty and interoperability loom as 3GPP pushes 6G toward a 2030 timeline
summary: As 3GPP advances 6G through the study phase, operators and vendors are grappling with AI uncertainty, interoperability and the risk of rushing standards to meet a 2030 target. Orange Group and NGMN’s Eric Hardouin explains where 6G standards stand — and what could derail them.

title: Amazon Leo promises commercial LEO service by mid-2026
summary: Amazon's CEO Andy Jassy said, “While Amazon Leo is officially scheduled to launch in mid-2026, we already have meaningful revenue commitments."

title: SBA Communications sale rumor buoys tower stocks
summary: Tower stocks rose this week on reports KKR and Brookfield are eyeing a possible buy of SBA Communications.

title: Opinion: Where is your AI off switch now?
summary: When AI is running everything, it can't be shut off because it would bring down everything, starting with the grid and working outwards and upwards from there. Control doesn’t have to be seized by the AI. It emerges from dependency.

title: Is Anthropic's Mythos a cybersecurity breakthrough, or just 'criti-hype'?
summary: Anthropic claims its Mythos AI model is too dangerous to release widely — but analysts say telcos should focus on security fundamentals, not the hype.

title: Opinion: AI runs on wireless infrastructure and policies must keep pace
summary: Industry leaders are already looking ahead to what comes next. As Nvidia, one of the world’s leading AI companies, has emphasized, future wireless generations will be inseparable from AI itself.

title: What are IPUs and why do they matter for AI?
summary: Intel’s IPUs are alternatives to the data processing units (DPUs) offered by Nvidia and AMD, and these kinds of chips will become increasingly important as inference workloads ramp up.

title: Starlink broadband speeds increase in 2025, but they’re still not great
summary: There are still a lot of areas in the U.S. where Starlink broadband doesn’t deliver the FCC’s minimum speeds of 100/20 Mbps, according to Ookla data.

title: Sovereign cloud and AI: Hidden tradeoffs and risks companies overlook
summary: Risks seem to fall into two main categories: physical risks tied to infrastructure and business risks related to a complicated and ever-changing global regulatory landscape.

title: The Five Nine: Inside data center financing—PE funding models and industry trends
summary: Private equity has aggressively poured billions into data centers and digital infrastructure in recent years.  But today, some investment assumptions are being stress tested.

title: Prepaid MVNO US Mobile teases ultimate bundle with Starlink
summary: US Mobile plans to launch a single bundle that combines mobile service across AT&T, Verizon and T-Mobile with Starlink home internet.

title: Cisco and the Shift to Autonomous AI-Driven Networks
summary: AI is rewriting network traffic and security, requiring service providers to adapt quickly to the changing environments. See how infrastructure is evolving in real time with Cisco. Watch now!

title: Anthropic snags Microsoft exec as new infrastructure chief
summary: The move comes as Anthropic presses ahead with major infrastructure initiatives, including a $50 billion data center push.

title: What’s stopping Anterix from launching D2D in 900 MHz?
summary: Grain Management wants the FCC to allow satellite D2D services in 800 MHz spectrum. So what’s to hold Anterix back from using its 900 MHz for D2D?

title: Skylo, Rakuten, Viasat, Telesat execs discuss satellite as a threat to telecom
summary: Will direct-to-device be complementary to telecom, or does it pose a major threat? We asked the “elephant in the room” question to satellite execs.

title: Globalstar expands private 5G push with next-gen XCOM RAN
summary: Globalstar isn’t commenting on speculation that it’s going to be bought. But it’s ready and willing to talk about its XCOM RAN portfolio.

title: Mediacom launches multi-gig and symmetrical services in Jefferson City, MO
summary: Mediacom has begun activating the new network on a neighborhood-by-neighborhood basis and expects to complete the entire Jefferson City area by the end of October.

title: Rising energy prices could spell trouble for telcos
summary: Little over a month in, the war in Iran is already having far-reaching impacts and now threatens to hit operators where it hurts: the bottom line.

title: Huawei Signals the Future of AI, 5G and 6G
summary: At MWC26, Huawei showcased how AI, 5G, and automation are driving real industrial scale across sectors. Watch now!

title: Q-Day just got closer — you need to be ready by 2029, Cloudflare says
summary: Cloudflare is accelerating its post-quantum roadmap as recent quantum computing breakthroughs suggest Q-Day is closer than you think

title: Grain Management wants to use 800 MHz for satellite D2D
summary: Private investment firm Grain Management wants to make 800 MHz spectrum available to satellite operators for direct-to-device (D2D) services.

title: Cisco sizing up the data center space race, says CEO Robbins
summary: Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins says the company is exploring how it can provide products for space-based data centers. "I wouldn't bet against Elon."

title: Telecom vendors face ‘challenging’ year as operator capex dips
summary: Wireless capital intensity is expected to drop significantly more in the mid-5G cycle than it did with 4G. Here's why and what it means for the market.

title: T-Mobile beats Verizon on brand image in TD Cowen survey
summary: After 13 years of TD Cowen surveys, T-Mobile finally took the No. 1 position on brand/image.

title: Career moves — Anthropic recruits Eric Boyd, former Microsoft exec
summary: Welcome to Career Moves, Fierce's weekly rundown of recent hirings, firings and retirings across the tech and telecom sectors.
